hero.title: Meme Generator
hero.tagline: Create fun memes by adding custom text to any image. Drag text anywhere on the canvas, adjust font size and color, then download your finished meme with one click.
hero.blogLink: Learn how to use the Meme Generator →

hint.text: Would you like to crop your image before creating a meme?

whatIs.heading: What is a Meme Generator
whatIs.body: A meme generator is a lightweight creative editor that lets you place text overlays on images to produce humorous, informative, or expressive graphics quickly. It supports multiple text layers, free positioning, font sizing, color selection, and simple export controls so you can craft a share-ready image in seconds.

howWorks.heading: How the generator works
howWorks.body: Upload or drop an image into the canvas area, then add one or more text layers using the controls. Each layer can be positioned by dragging, resized via the advanced controls, and styled with a color picker. The preview area reflects changes in real time.

whyBrowser.heading: Why use a browser-based tool
whyBrowser.body: Browser-based meme editors are instant and accessible: they don't require installations, run offline once loaded, and keep your images local to your device.

tips.heading: Tips for better memes
tips.item1: Use short, punchy captions and capitalize text for classic meme styles.
tips.item2: Keep good contrast between text and background; add stroke or shadow if needed for readability.
tips.item3: Use multiple layers for complex layouts—title, subtitle, or small annotations all work well.

faq.heading: FAQs
faq.q1: Q: Will my image be uploaded anywhere?
faq.a1: A: No — everything runs client-side in your browser.
faq.q2: Q: Can I add more than two text lines?
faq.a2: A: Yes — use the ＋ button to add as many text layers as you need.
faq.q3: Q: What image formats are supported?
faq.a3: A: Any image format your browser supports (JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF, etc.).

howItWorks.step1: Upload an image by clicking the canvas area or dragging and dropping a file onto it.
howItWorks.step2: Click the ＋ button to add a text layer and type your caption in the text box on the left panel.
howItWorks.step3: Drag the text overlay on the canvas to position it. Use the Advanced section to adjust font size and color.
howItWorks.step4: Add more text layers as needed, then click Download to save your meme as a PNG.

canvas.placeholder: Click or drop image here to upload
canvas.previewHint: Drag to pan · Alt+Scroll to zoom · Pinch on mobile

guide.title: How to Create Memes That Actually Go Viral (Simple Guide for Beginners)
guide.intro: Memes are everywhere. From social media feeds to group chats, memes have become one of the fastest ways to communicate ideas, humor, and opinions.

learnItems.item2: How to create one from scratch
learnItems.item4: Tips to make your memes more engaging

whatIs.heading: What Is a Meme (Really)?
whatIs.body: A meme is a piece of content — usually an image with text — designed to be shared and adapted by others. Unlike regular images, memes are relatable, quick to understand, and easy to share.
whatIs.tip: 👉 A good meme delivers its message in seconds.

why.heading: Why Memes Are So Popular
why.item1: Instant Communication — Memes compress ideas into one image and a few words, faster than paragraphs.
why.item2: Relatability — The best memes make people think "That's exactly me."
why.item3: Shareability — Memes are designed to be reposted and modified.
why.item4: Low Effort, High Impact — You don't need design skills or expensive tools; just a good idea.

anatomy.heading: Anatomy of a Good Meme
anatomy.image: Image: Recognizable or expressive, supports the message.
anatomy.text: Text: Short and clear, easy to read.
anatomy.punchline: Punchline: The twist or humor that makes people share.

types.heading: Types of Memes You Can Create
types.item4: 🧠 Informational Memes — mix humor with useful info

bestPractices.heading: Best Practices
bestPractices.item1: Keep text short — 2–3 seconds to read.
bestPractices.item2: Use clear, bold fonts with high contrast.
bestPractices.item3: Match text to image so it reinforces the message.
bestPractices.item4: Stay relevant — trending formats perform better.
bestPractices.item5: Know your audience and tailor the humor.

mistakes.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
mistakes.item5: Trying too hard to be funny

stepByStep.heading: Step-by-Step: How to Create a Meme

useCases.heading: Real-World Use Cases
useCases.item4: 👥 Group Chats — react faster than typing

tips.heading: Tips to Make Your Memes Stand Out

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.a1: No — just a good idea and clear message.
faq.a2: You can, but be mindful of copyright and prefer common meme formats when possible.
faq.q3: Why are my memes not getting engagement?
faq.a3: Possible reasons: too much text, not relatable, outdated format.

guide.conclusion: Creating memes isn't about complex design — it's about communication and timing. Focus on clear ideas, simple text, and relatability.
guide.lead2: But creating a meme that people actually share? That’s a different story.
